hero.label: Secure Data Transfer
hero.title:
Move data <1>directly</1>.
No cloud.
No middleman.
hero.description: Mirall connects your devices directly. Create a space, invite collaborators, and move files that travel straight between devices — encrypted end-to-end, and completely serverless.
hero.betaNotice: Mirall is currently in beta. Some features may change and you might encounter bugs.
hero.industries: Trusted for post-production, architecture, legal, and research workflows.

features.title: Built different
features.description: No accounts. No uploads to someone else's server. Mirall gives you a private space to share files the way it should work.

spaces.title: Shared Spaces
spaces.description: Create topic-based spaces for your team, project, or group. Everyone in a space sees all available files and chooses what to download.

direct.title: Direct Device-to-Device
direct.description: Files move straight between your devices over encrypted connections. No server ever sees your data.

encrypted.title: Encrypted by Default
encrypted.description: Every connection between devices is end-to-end encrypted. No keys to manage — it just works.

inviteOnly.title: Invite-Only Access
inviteOnly.description: Share a simple invite code to add people to a space. No accounts, no sign-ups, no permission dashboards.

explicitDownload.title: You Choose What to Keep
explicitDownload.description: See every file available in a space. Download only what you need — nothing syncs without your say-so.

zeroInfra.title: Zero Infrastructure
zeroInfra.description: No cloud storage fees. No server maintenance. Mirall is fully decentralized — files move directly between your devices.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.description: Three steps. No setup wizards, no admin panels.

items.title: Create a space
items.description: Name it, pick an icon, and you're live. Mirall generates a unique invite code you can share with anyone.

items.title: Invite your people
items.description: Send the invite code. Your collaborators paste it in and they're connected — directly to you, no server in between.

items.title: Share and download
items.description: Drop files into the space. Everyone sees what's available. Each person downloads exactly what they need.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Everything you need to know about Mirall.

items.question: Where are my files stored?
items.answer: On your device, and only your device. When you share a file, other members download it directly from you. There is no cloud server, no intermediary storage. Once someone downloads a file, they have their own local copy.

items.question: What happens when I'm offline?
items.answer: Files you've downloaded stay on your device — they're yours. Other members can still see the file metadata, but they can't download files from you until you're back online. If another member who has the same file is online, they can download from them instead.

items.question: Is it really encrypted?
items.answer: Yes. Every connection between devices is end-to-end encrypted. No one — not even us — can see your files or metadata in transit. There's no central server that could be breached.

items.question: How do I invite someone to a space?
items.answer: When you create a space, Mirall generates an invite code. Share it however you like — chat, email, text. They paste it into the app and they're connected instantly.

items.question: Are there file size limits?
items.answer: No hard limits. Mirall streams files in chunks, so large files work fine. Transfer speed depends on your network connection and the sender's upload bandwidth.

items.question: Is Mirall free?
items.answer: Mirall is free to use for now, but we plan to charge for it in the future.

items.question: How is Mirall different from Aspera, MASV, or WeTransfer?
items.answer: Those services all route or store your files on their own infrastructure, which means per-GB fees, transfer ceilings, and a cloud vendor in the middle of your data. Mirall transfers files directly between devices with no servers in between — so there are no per-GB costs, no transfer caps, and nothing to breach.

items.question: Is Mirall suitable for GDPR, Schrems II, or HIPAA workflows?
items.answer: Yes. Because Mirall never stores your files on a third-party server, there is no cross-border data transfer or cloud processor relationship to worry about — the jurisdictional question doesn't apply. Many regulated teams in healthcare, legal, and post-production use this as an architectural answer to GDPR, Schrems II, and HIPAA concerns.

download.label: Download
download.title: Download Mirall
download.description: Available for macOS, Windows, and Linux. Pick your platform and start moving data directly between devices in seconds.
download.betaNotice: This is a beta release — we are looking for your feedback.

cta.title: Your files. Your devices. No middleman.
cta.description: Join the movement that share files the way it should be — direct, encrypted, and free from cloud lock-in.

footer.tagline: Secure data transfer for teams. No cloud, no accounts, no compromises.

support.title: Support
support.heading: Get help from inside the app
support.description: Mirall has built-in support. Click the "Send Feedback" button in the top right corner of the app to report issues, request features, or ask questions. Your message goes directly to our team.
support.screenshotAlt: Mirall app with the Send Feedback button highlighted

steps.title: How to reach us
items: Click the "Send Feedback" button in the top right corner
Hit send — we'll get back to you

support.note: No external ticketing system, no email chains. Everything stays in the app.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.heading: Getting started with Mirall
docs.intro: Mirall is a secure data transfer app for desktop. No cloud, no accounts — files move directly between you and the people you invite. This guide walks you through everything the app can do.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.description: The first time you open Mirall, it asks for a display name and an optional avatar. That's your profile — it's how other members will see you inside any space you join or create. You can change both later in Settings.
gettingStarted.screenshotAlt: Mirall onboarding screen asking for a display name and optional avatar

spaces.title: Shared Spaces
spaces.description: Spaces are private groups where people share files with each other. Every space has its own members and its own files, and the list of spaces you belong to lives on the "Shared Spaces" home screen. You can filter the list by All Spaces or Favorites, and star any space to pin it to Favorites.
spaces.screenshotAlt: Mirall Shared Spaces screen with space cards and create/join buttons

creating.title: Creating a space
steps: Click "Create Space" on the Shared Spaces screen
Give the space a name and pick an icon
Click "Initialize Space" — Mirall generates an invite code and shows it right away
Copy the invite code and send it to anyone you want to add

joining.title: Joining a space
steps: Click "Join Space" on the Shared Spaces screen
Paste the invite code you received
Optionally give the space a local name — only you will see it
Click "Join Space" — once another member is online, their files appear automatically

managing.title: Managing a space
managing.description: Open a space and use the More menu next to "Invite to Space" to add it to Favorites, edit its name or icon, or leave it. The member avatars on each space card show you at a glance who's active.

files.title: Sharing files
files.description: Inside a space you see every file every member has shared. Nothing downloads automatically — metadata travels between devices so you know what's available, and you decide which files to actually pull onto your device.
files.screenshotAlt: Mirall Space View showing shared files, drop zone, storage indicator, and members

uploading.title: Adding files
uploading.description: Drag files onto the "Drop to Mirror" zone in the sidebar, or click "Select Files" to pick them from your computer. Mirall adds them to the space and starts making them available to other members right away. Very large files (up to 1TB) work fine — content is streamed in chunks, so there's no upload step to wait through.

downloading.title: Downloading files
downloading.description: Click the download button on any file shared by another member. The file transfers directly from their device to yours with live progress, speed, and ETA. If multiple members have the same file, Mirall pulls from whoever is available.

transfers.title: Transfers that resume
transfers.description: If your network drops or the member you're downloading from goes offline, the transfer pauses automatically. It picks up from where it left off as soon as another member with that file comes back — you don't need to restart anything. A file that's half-downloaded but no longer wanted can be discarded with one click.

items.meaning: A file you added to the space. It lives on your device and is offered to other members.
A file you downloaded from another member. It's yours to keep and works offline.
Another member is sharing this file and at least one copy is online. Click download to pull it.
A transfer is in progress. The card shows bytes, speed, and ETA.
Mirall is finishing preparing a file you just added so other members can fetch it.
A partial download paused due to a network issue. Click play to resume.
Every member who has this file is currently offline. It will become available when one reconnects.
A transfer errored out. Click retry to try again, or dismiss it.

actions.title: File actions
items: Reveal in folder — open a downloaded or shared file in your OS file browser
Unshare — remove a file you added so it's no longer offered to other members (files they've already downloaded stay on their devices)
Pause — stop an in-progress download; its progress is kept so you can resume later
Resume — continue an interrupted download from where it stopped
Discard partial — throw away a paused or failed download and free the space
Retry — reattempt a download that ended in an error

members.title: Members and connections
members.description: Every space has a Members panel on the right showing everyone else in it. You'll see their name, avatar, and whether they're currently online. When someone is online you can download from them; when everyone offering a given file is offline, that file is marked accordingly until someone comes back.

inviting.title: Sending an invite
inviting.description: Click "Invite to Space" at the top of a space to open the invite dialog, then copy the code and send it to whoever you want to add — chat, email, anything works. They paste it into Mirall's Join Space flow and they're in. The same code can be shared with as many people as you like.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: The gear icon in the top right opens Settings. From here you can manage your profile, switch between light and dark mode, and drill into storage or app info.
settings.screenshotAlt: Mirall Settings screen showing profile, appearance, and general options
items: Profile — update your display name and avatar at any time; changes sync to spaces automatically
Appearance — toggle between light and dark theme
General — open the Storage or About subpages for more detail

storage.title: Storage
storage.description: The Storage page shows how much disk Mirall is using on this device and breaks it down per space. For each space you see the cached content vs. metadata split. Only the cache is counted — files you've already saved to your Downloads folder aren't included, and leaving a space automatically clears its cache.

about.title: About
about.description: The About page shows the installed version and links to documentation and support so you can always find your way back here.

privacy.title: Privacy and security
privacy.description: Every connection between devices is end-to-end encrypted. Your files never pass through a server — they travel directly between devices. There are no accounts and no tracking, and only the people you invite can see what you share.

leaving.title: Leaving a space
leaving.description: To leave a space, open it, open the More menu, and choose Leave Space. Mirall cancels any in-flight transfers and removes the space's cached data from your device. Files you've already saved to your Downloads folder are untouched — they stay yours.
